source_environment: VS Code integrated browser (Playwright a11y snapshot via read_page/navigate_page)
notes: LinkedIn ships obfuscated, hashed CSS class names AND sometimes omits role/aria-label on the global search input. Never depend on CSS classes.
The accessibility tree (role + accessible name) is the most reliable layer and is what read_page returns.
Href patterns are the most stable identifiers for canonical entities (/in/, /company/, /feed/update/urn:li:activity:).
Interface accessible names were observed in German (de-DE); match all locales via linkedin-i18n.json.

selectors.purpose: Confirm an authenticated session by presence of the primary nav (Me/Sie menu).
selectors.postcondition: Primary global navigation is present and URL is not a /login or /checkpoint page.
selectors.notes: Feed reported lang=en but nav accessible names were German (Start, Ihr Netzwerk, Sie).

selectors.purpose: Enter a search query.
selectors.postcondition: Query text appears in the field; submitting opens /search/results/.
selectors.notes: role/aria-label were null on the raw input; the a11y tree exposes it as textbox 'Suche' inside a search landmark. Prefer direct results-URL navigation over typing.

selectors.purpose: Open People results directly by URL (most robust read-only navigation).
selectors.postcondition: URL contains /search/results/people/ and result cards render.
selectors.notes: Additional URL params supported: geoUrn/currentCompany/keywords. Verified live.

selectors.purpose: Open Companies results directly by URL.
selectors.postcondition: URL contains /search/results/companies/ and company cards render.
selectors.notes: Verified live with keyword 'Verpackungsmaschinen'.

selectors.purpose: Open Posts/Content results directly by URL, with date sort and recency filters applied via query params (NO filter-pill clicking required).
selectors.postcondition: URL contains /search/results/content/ and post cards render, sorted newest-first and constrained to the chosen recency window.
selectors.notes: VERIFIED LIVE 2026-07-12. Filtering by URL query is strictly preferred over clicking the filter pills. Verified params: sortBy=%22date_posted%22 (Aktuellste/most-recent; the other value is %22relevance%22), datePosted=%22past-week%22 (other verified-shape values: %22past-24h%22, %22past-month%22). Values MUST keep the URL-encoded double quotes (%22...%22). origin=FACETED_SEARCH is what the UI itself sets. Optional facets appended the same way: contentType, authorCompany, authorIndustry, fromMember, fromOrganization. This makes recency-scoped harvesting deterministic without touching the Beiträge/Aktuellste/Veröffentlichungsdatum pills.

selectors.purpose: Canonical, verified query-parameter vocabulary for content search — the robust alternative to indexing the obfuscated filter-pill selectors.
selectors.postcondition: Results reflect the requested sort and recency without any pill interaction.
selectors.notes: The filter pills (Beiträge, Aktuellste, Veröffentlichungsdatum, Inhaltsart, Vom Mitglied, Alle Filter, Zurücksetzen) render with fully hashed CSS classes and componentkey UUIDs, so DO NOT index them; drive the same state through the URL instead. Decision: URL query = best; selector indexing of pills = avoid.

selectors.purpose: Locate each post card in the NEW content-search UI and extract the directly-available author/company identity.

primary.note: Exactly one control-menu button per post card; climb to the smallest ancestor that still contains exactly one such button plus an a[href*='/in/'] to isolate the card.

selectors.postcondition: Per card: author name + /in/ URL, optional /company/ URL, relative date span, and full post text are extracted from the DOM.
selectors.notes: NEW UI CRITICAL FINDING (2026-07-12): the post card DOM/React props expose the AUTHOR (urn:li:member:{id}, /in/ link) and company, but NOT the post's own urn:li:activity id and NO /feed/update/ href. The relative-time is a plain <span> with no link. To get the canonical permalink use post_permalink_via_control_menu. Cards lazy-load; scroll to render more.

selectors.purpose: Read-only extraction of a post's canonical activity id in the new UI, where it is absent from the card DOM.

fallbacks.note: Same href also yields entityUrn=urn:li:share:{id} and authorUrn=urn:li:member:{id}; then press Escape to close the menu.

selectors.postcondition: Canonical URL https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:{id}/ is reconstructed. The Report control is NEVER clicked — only its href attribute is read — then the menu is closed with Escape.
selectors.notes: Verified live 2026-07-12: opening 'Kontrollmenü für den Beitrag von {Autor} öffnen' renders a menu whose 'Beitrag melden' item has href .../report-in-modal/?entityUrn=urn:li:share:...&authorUrn=urn:li:member:...&updateUrn=urn:li:activity:.... Reading that href is read-only; do NOT activate Report/Save/Copy. This is the ONLY reliable in-UI source of the activity id for content-search results.

selectors.purpose: Locate each person result card and its canonical profile URL.
selectors.postcondition: Each card yields a canonical linkedin.com/in/{slug}/ URL, a name, and a headline line.
selectors.notes: People cards use ARIA role=list/role=listitem on divs (not ul/li). Card text = name + connection degree ('• 2.') + headline.

selectors.purpose: Locate each company result card and its canonical company URL.
selectors.postcondition: Each card yields a canonical linkedin.com/company/{slug}/ URL and a company name.
selectors.notes: Company results observed inside a ul with ~6 li per page.

selectors.purpose: Detect the empty-state so the skill reports zero results honestly instead of hanging.
selectors.postcondition: Empty-state heading present; result list absent.
selectors.notes: Exact phrase 'Verpackungsmaschinen Baden-Württemberg' returned this empty state.

selectors.purpose: Advance to the next results page (bounded by max_result_pages).
selectors.postcondition: Result list updates; page index increases.

selectors.purpose: Go back to the previous results page.
selectors.postcondition: Result list updates; page index decreases.

selectors.purpose: Switch between company tabs (read-only navigation).
selectors.postcondition: Company sub-page loads at /company/{slug}/{tab}/.
selectors.notes: Verified tabs: Start, Info, Beiträge, Personen, Einblicke.

selectors.purpose: Read structured overview fields from the About section.

fallbacks.note: Match dt label text against linkedin-i18n.company.*

selectors.postcondition: Label/value pairs are extracted for Branche/Größe/Hauptsitz/Website/Gegründet/Spezialgebiete.
selectors.notes: Verified live: Branche='Maschinenbau & Betriebstechnik', Größe='1.001 bis 5.000 Beschäftigte', Hauptsitz='Crailsheim, Baden-Württemberg'.

selectors.purpose: Read the profile owner's display name.
selectors.postcondition: A non-empty display name is obtained.
selectors.notes: Plain document.querySelector('h1') was empty on some renders; document.title is the robust fallback.

selectors.purpose: Anchor to profile sections for read-only extraction.
selectors.postcondition: The requested section element is located by its heading.
selectors.notes: Verified section headings: Aktivitäten, Erfahrung, Ausbildung, Kenntnisse, Interessen.

selectors.purpose: Locate a post and derive its canonical activity URL.
selectors.postcondition: Canonical URL https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:{id}/ is derivable.
selectors.notes: Verified urn:li:activity:7480901060509773826 on a company posts feed.

selectors.purpose: Read the visible relative timestamp of a post.
selectors.postcondition: A relative date string is captured and normalized via lib/normalize.mjs.
selectors.notes: Observed: '3 Tage •', '1 Woche(n) •', '8 Monat(e) •', '3 Woche(n) • Bearbeitet •'.

selectors.purpose: Read visible reaction/comment/repost counts as context only.
selectors.postcondition: Numeric counts captured; no interaction with reaction controls.
selectors.notes: Observed: '16 Reaktionen', '1 Kommentar'. WARNING: adjacent controls 'Mit „Gefällt mir" reagieren', 'Kommentieren', 'Reposten', 'Senden' are FORBIDDEN.

selectors.purpose: Collect visible commenters' names and profile URLs.
selectors.postcondition: Commenter name + canonical /in/ URL captured, excluding the post author and the session user.
selectors.notes: Comment containers use obfuscated classes; may require expanding the comments count first. Filter out the authenticated session user.

selectors.purpose: Open the full filter panel.
selectors.postcondition: Filter dialog opens.
selectors.notes: NOT verified live. Prefer applying filters via URL params (geoUrn, currentCompany, industry) where possible.

selectors.purpose: Filter results by location.
selectors.postcondition: Results constrained to the chosen location.
selectors.notes: NOT verified live.

selectors.purpose: Filter people by current company.
selectors.postcondition: Results constrained to the chosen company.
selectors.notes: NOT verified live.

selectors.purpose: Filter results by industry when available.
selectors.postcondition: Results constrained to the chosen industry.
selectors.notes: NOT verified live; may not exist for all accounts.

selectors.purpose: Open Events results when available.
selectors.postcondition: Event results render.
selectors.notes: NOT verified live.

selectors.purpose: Search employees within a company People tab.
selectors.postcondition: Employee list filters by query.
selectors.notes: NOT verified live.

selectors.purpose: INTENTIONALLY NOT USED. Listed to explicitly forbid opening contact info.
selectors.postcondition: Never invoked. Emails/phones must never be collected.
selectors.notes: The skill must never open 'Kontaktinfo'/'Contact info' nor extract emails/phones.
